ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sensor_types" (
  "sensor_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor type */,
  "sensor_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the sensor type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor type */,
  "sensor_model" TEXT /* Model of the sensor */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the sensor */,
  "specifications" TEXT /* Detailed specifications of the sensor */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date when the sensor was last calibrated */,
  "calibration_needed" INTEGER /* Indicates if the sensor needs calibration */,
  "last_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date when the sensor was last maintained */,
  "next_maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date when the next maintenance is due */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the data source */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "sensor_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor type */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date when the data source was installed */,
  "last_check_date" TEXT /* Date when the data source was last checked */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the data source */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Additional notes about the data source */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_sources_sensor_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_type_id") REFERENCES sensor_types ("sensor_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_types" (
  "data_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data type */,
  "data_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the data type */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data type */,
  "processing_method" TEXT /* Method used to process the data */,
  "processing_time" REAL /* Time taken to process the data */,
  "quality_score" REAL /* Quality score of the processed data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, data scientist, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "password_salt" TEXT /* Salt used for hashing the password */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Date and time of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Indicates if the user account is active */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "access_level" INTEGER /* Access level of the user */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data version */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* ID of the data record */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the data record */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was created */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the changes in this version */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who created the version */,
  "version_type" TEXT /* Type of version (e.g., major, minor, patch) */,
  "rollback_possible" INTEGER /* Indicates if the version can be rolled back */,
  "rollback_date" TEXT /* Date when the version was rolled back (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_versions_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
